author: catwith1hat
content:
Good morning, I am publishing my LLM-assisted execution client audit today: https://github.com/catwith1hat/evm-breaker/ . I tasked an agent to find consensus breakdowns across reth, erigon, geth, ethrex, besu, and nethermind.

Surprisingly to me, the agent found 3 issues easily reachable from mainnet for one client (now remediated). The audit also found many dormant issues, e.g. gas overflow computations for implausibly high fee levels, and revert inconsistencies across predeployed system contracts. I also extended the analysis to bal-devnet-6 code. It identified around 9 consensus issues in new glamsterdam code.

I'd love feedback on whether this work is useful and/or whether the issues flagged are true/false positives.
